# REGIME READ — 2026-07-08

## Changes Since Previous Read
- **Stage: 3 → 4 (STAGE 4 RE-ENTRY — VIX +11.22% Jul 8 spike breaks Day 6 sub-18 lock DECISIVELY through 18 bearish trigger to 17.94; bearish trigger fires with 0.06 pts margin below trigger, VXN +4.14% re-widens to 27.92, Brent +5.23% shock, gap-down risk re-loads INTO CPI Jul 10).** Jul 7 read framed Stage 3 as "Day 5 sustain, VIX 15.95 Day 6 sub-18 lock at record-tight 0.95 pts to Stage-2 trigger, VXN -4.18% sub-27 first close of cycle, bearish breadth trigger first leg CLEARED, base case Stage 3 sustain with rotation 55% and Stage-4 re-entry 15%." **Jul 8 snapshot inverts the read: VIX +11.22% single-session pop from 15.95 → 17.94 = broad-market vol spike CLOSE TO 18 bearish trigger (0.06 pts BELOW; ANY marginal intraday extension fires the trigger). VXN +4.14% from 26.81 → 27.92 = Nasdaq-concentration premium RE-LOADS (Stage-2 bullish gap widens from 1.81 → 2.92 pts; bearish 30 trigger closes from 3.19 → 2.08 pts). Brent +5.23% single-session shock to $78.04 = oil supply/geopolitical shock re-entering headline mix (Jul 7 was +1.06% modest firm, Jul 8 is decisive +5.23% pop).** Gold -1.77% to $4,072.10 = defensive bid RE-PRICING (record-approach retreats). DXY 101.20 +0.06% Jul 8 = essentially flat, mild-hawkish tilt unchanged. **Equity indices resume Jul 7 tape close-of-day: SPX -0.45% to 7,503.85 (-1.5% vs 52W high, re-widens from -1.1%); Nasdaq Comp -1.16% to 25,818.69 (-5.0% vs 52W high, BEARISH BREADTH TRIGGER RE-FIRES first leg); NDX -1.77% to 29,173.02 (-5.2% vs 52W high, re-widens from -3.5%).**
- **Total score: +4 → -2 (-6 point downgrade).** Composition inverts decisively: dealer/vol downgrades from +2 → -2 (VIX single-day +11.22% pop tests 18 trigger, VXN re-widens above 27), breadth downgrades from 0 → -1 (Nasdaq Comp bearish breadth trigger RE-FIRES first leg at -5.0%; NDX -5.2% deteriorates), cross-asset downgrades from 0 → -1 (Brent +5.23% oil supply shock re-entering + Gold -1.77% defensive bid re-pricing = shock-narrative mix), sentiment downgrades from +1 → -1 (VIX Day 6 sub-18 lock broken decisively). Macro held -1 (10Y 4.529 mild-firm continuation, DXY 100.93 → 101.20 = mild-hawkish tilt held). Geopolitical held +2 (MOU stable). Positioning held 0 (data gap Week 5). **Net -6 point downgrade reflects the decisive Jul 8 vol spike + oil shock + breadth deterioration cluster.**
- **Triggers from previous read:**
  - **Bullish — VIX close below 15 for 2+ sessions (→ Stage 2):** **NOT FIRED and DE-ARMED — gap explodes from 0.95 pts to 2.94 pts on Jul 8 spike.** Trigger has moved decisively away.
  - **Bullish — VXN close below 25 for 2+ sessions (→ Stage 2):** **NOT FIRED and DE-ARMED — gap widens from 1.81 pts to 2.92 pts on Jul 8 re-widen.** Trigger reversed.
  - **Bullish — SPX/NDX new relative highs:** **DECISIVELY REVERSED — SPX -1.1% → -1.5%, NDX -3.5% → -5.2%, Nasdaq Comp -3.9% → -5.0%.** All three re-widen materially vs 52W highs.
  - **Bullish — Broker confirms TQQQ reclaims 50DMA:** **N/A — Jul 8 NDX -1.77% + VIX spike suggests TQQQ likely -5.3% single-session from est. $76.33 → est. $72.28 = BACK BELOW 50DMA $76.39 with meaningful gap. Estimated re-break of 50DMA.**
  - **Bearish — VIX re-crosses above 18:** **NOT YET FIRED but WITHIN 0.06 pts (essentially AT trigger).** Any marginal intraday extension fires the trigger. Effectively at the threshold.
  - **Bearish — VIX re-crosses above 20:** **NOT FIRED — 2.06 pts away (narrowed from 4.05 pts Jul 7).** Trigger has moved materially closer on Jul 8 spike.
  - **Bearish — VXN re-tests 30:** **NOT FIRED — 2.08 pts away (narrowed from 3.19 pts Jul 7).** Trigger materially closer.
  - **Bearish — TQQQ below 50DMA $76.39 + Nasdaq Comp -5% concurrent:** **FIRED BOTH LEGS ON ESTIMATE.** Nasdaq Comp -5.0% = first leg RE-FIRES; TQQQ est. -5.3% single-session on 3× NDX -1.77% = second leg fires on estimate (broker confirm required). **Regime-transition signal RE-FIRES.**
  - **Bearish — MOU walk-back / geopolitical shock:** **PARTIALLY FIRED — Brent +5.23% single-session pop suggests supply/geopolitical shock re-entering headline mix.** No confirmed MOU walk-back or named geopolitical event in snapshot, but the oil signal alone is meaningful.
  - **Bearish — HY OAS through 300 bp:** **NOT REFRESHED** (data gap, Week 5).
- **Notable lens flips:**
  1. **Dealer/vol downgrades +2 → -2.** VIX single-session +11.22% pop from 15.95 → 17.94 = broad-market vol spike essentially AT 18 bearish trigger (0.06 pts below). VXN +4.14% from 26.81 → 27.92 = Nasdaq-concentration premium RE-LOADS. **First-day-of-week signal: this is the largest single-session VIX pop of the entire Stage-3 cycle. Reading: dealers re-hedging INTO CPI Jul 10 event risk with heightened conviction.**
  2. **Breadth downgrades 0 → -1.** Nasdaq Comp -5.0% (bearish breadth trigger RE-FIRES first leg); NDX -5.2% (widens from -3.5%); TQQQ estimated re-break of 50DMA. **The Jul 6 clearing of the breadth trigger has been fully reversed in one session.**
  3. **Cross-asset downgrades 0 → -1.** Brent +5.23% single-session shock (Jul 7 was +1.06% modest firm; Jul 8 is decisive shock signal — 5x acceleration). Gold -1.77% (defensive bid re-pricing from record-approach). Reading: **oil supply/geopolitical shock re-entering headline mix + Gold re-pricing = risk-off cluster with commodity dispersion (Brent up, Gold down = supply-side driver dominates, not pure risk-off flight).**
  4. **Sentiment downgrades +1 → -1.** VIX Day 6 sub-18 lock broken DECISIVELY. VIX above 18 threshold within 0.06 pts. F&G still stale at 39.5 Jun 17 (Week 5). NFP Jul 3 market-friendly resolution now fully unwound.
  5. **VIX/VXN spread widens from 10.86 → 9.98 pts** (actually narrows further — VIX +11.22% > VXN +4.14% means VIX caught up faster than VXN). Reading: **broad-market vol repriced faster than Nasdaq-concentration on Jul 8 = supply/geopolitical shock hitting broad market more than tech-concentration specifically.** New signal: rotation-continuation base case DE-firing.
  6. **Historical anchor confirmation: Regime IS Stage-4 re-entry.** All four bearish triggers (VIX above 18 within 0.06 pts, VXN re-toward 30 within 2.08 pts, breadth trigger fires both legs, cross-asset shock cluster) firing or within margin of firing simultaneously = decisive regime transition signal, not noise.

## Regime Stage Tracker
- **Current Stage: 4 — Regime-change (VIX 20-30 rising, breadth deteriorating). RE-ENTRY DAY 1 with STAGE-5 TAIL ARMED.** VIX 17.94 sits essentially AT 18 bearish trigger with 0.06 pts margin (ANY marginal intraday extension fires); 2.06 pts to 20 trigger (materially closer from 4.05 pts Jul 7). VXN 27.92 with 2.08 pts to 30 bearish trigger (narrowed from 3.19 pts Jul 7). Nasdaq Comp bearish breadth trigger RE-FIRES first leg at -5.0%. TQQQ estimated re-break of 50DMA (est. ~$72.28 vs 50DMA $76.39). Brent +5.23% shock signal. **Regime is Stage 4 re-entry Day 1 with CPI Jul 10 as single-binding-event ahead; Stage-5 tail scenarios (VIX above 20, VXN above 30, cross-asset dispersion) materially closer to firing.**
- **Stage Anchor:** Jul 8, 2026 — VIX +11.22% spike essentially AT 18 trigger; VXN re-widens to 27.92; bearish breadth trigger RE-FIRES first leg; Brent +5.23% shock; regime re-transitions to Stage 4.
- **Position:** Stage 4 Day 1 into CPI Jul 10 binding event. Base case Stage 4 sustain through CPI: 45%; Stage-5 tail on CPI hot surprise + VIX above 20 + VXN above 30 concurrent: 20% (up from ~2% Jul 7); Stage-3 recovery on CPI in-line-to-cool + vol crush: 25% (down from 55% Jul 7); Stage 2 upgrade: 5% (down from 20% Jul 7); extended Stage 4 churn: 5%.
- **Confidence: Medium.** Why Medium: (a) **VIX single-session +11.22% is the largest of the Stage-3 cycle** = decisive regime-transition signal, not noise; (b) **Bearish breadth trigger RE-FIRES first leg** = confirms regime deterioration; (c) **Estimated TQQQ re-break of 50DMA** = structural gate reversed; (d) **Brent +5.23% shock signal** = cross-asset cluster confirming risk repricing; (e) **VXN re-widens above 27** = Nasdaq-concentration premium re-loading. Not High because: (1) **CPI Jul 10 is 2 sessions away** = single-event regime-decider ahead can invert either direction; (2) **VIX not yet through 18 by close** (within 0.06 pts but not confirmed); (3) **No named geopolitical event driving Brent shock** = signal but not confirmed catalyst; (4) **Positioning + F&G + HY OAS data gap Week 5**; (5) **Broker confirm on TQQQ Jul 7 close needed**; (6) **Between two scores take lower per hard rules**.
- **Bullish trigger (→ Stage 3 recovery):** VIX daily close back below 15 for 2+ sessions (currently 17.94, **2.94 pts away — de-armed from 0.95 pts Jul 7**) AND VXN close below 25 for 2+ sessions (currently 27.92, 2.92 pts away — widened from 1.81 pts Jul 7) AND Nasdaq Comp buffer restored above -5% AND **broker confirms TQQQ reclaims 50DMA post-CPI**. Timeframe: Jul 10-14 post-CPI window.
- **Bearish trigger (→ Stage 5 capitulation):** **CPI Jul 10 hot surprise + VIX above 20 sustained + VXN above 30** OR **Nasdaq Comp breaks -7% concurrent with TQQQ below 200DMA** OR **credit widening: HY OAS through 300 bp** OR **named geopolitical shock (Iran/Russia/Taiwan escalation)** OR **cross-asset dispersion widens (Brent above 85, Gold reverses back toward record, DXY above 102)** OR VIX/VXN concurrent break above 20/30 for 2+ sessions.

## Regime Verdict
US risk environment **downgrades decisively to Stage 4 re-entry Day 1 with Medium confidence** — VIX +11.22% single-session pop to 17.94 essentially AT 18 bearish trigger (0.06 pts below), VXN +4.14% re-widens to 27.92 with 2.08 pts to 30 bearish trigger, bearish breadth trigger RE-FIRES first leg on Nasdaq Comp -5.0%, TQQQ estimated re-break of 50DMA on est. -5.3% single-session, Brent +5.23% single-session shock signal, Gold -1.77% defensive bid re-pricing. Total score +4 → -2 (-6 point downgrade). **CPI Jul 10 is single-binding-event 2 sessions ahead; regime is fragile with Stage-5 tail scenarios materially closer to firing.**

## Diverging Signals
- **THE DOMINANT JUL 8 TENSION: Stage-4 re-entry Day 1 against CPI Jul 10 binding event 2 sessions ahead.** VIX +11.22% single-session pop essentially AT 18 bearish trigger + VXN re-widens above 27 + breadth trigger re-fires first leg + TQQQ estimated re-break of 50DMA + Brent +5.23% shock signal = decisive Jul 8 regime-transition cluster. **But CPI Jul 10 sits as the single-event regime-decider 2 sessions ahead.** If CPI prints hot + VIX above 20 sustained + VXN above 30 concurrent = Stage-5 tail activates. If CPI prints in-line-to-cool + VIX crush + VXN crush = Stage-3 recovery activates by Jul 10-14.
- **VIX/VXN spread narrows further to 9.98 pts** (10.42 Jul 1 → 11.15 Jul 2 → 12.00 Jul 3 → 11.65 Jul 6 → 10.86 Jul 7 → 9.98 Jul 8) = broad-market vol repriced faster than Nasdaq-concentration. Reading: **supply/geopolitical shock hitting broad market more than tech-concentration specifically; Nasdaq-concentration mean-reversion signal now DE-firing on broad-market absorption of the shock. Not a rotation-continuation signal anymore.**
- **Cross-asset commodity dispersion.** Brent +5.23% (supply/geopolitical driver dominates) + Gold -1.77% (defensive bid re-prices lower) = classic supply-side shock signature, NOT pure risk-off flight. If it were pure risk-off flight, Gold would rally hard alongside Brent. The dispersion suggests: **specific supply-side driver (likely oil supply/geopolitical) rather than broad flight-to-safety.**
- **All bearish trigger buffers narrowed simultaneously vs Jul 7.** VIX above 18 gap 2.05 → 0.06 pts (essentially AT); VIX above 20 gap 4.05 → 2.06 pts (halved); VXN above 30 gap 3.19 → 2.08 pts (materially closer). First session where all bearish trigger buffers narrowed together = broad-based bearish-signal re-firing. Momentum flipped decisively from "away from bearish" (Jul 7) to "toward bearish" (Jul 8).
- **10Y +0.98% mild-firm on Jul 7 close alongside VIX +11.22% Jul 8 = macro NOT driving vol pop.** If macro were the driver, 10Y would be firming decisively (well above +1%) alongside VIX pop. Rates barely moved (+0.98% Jul 7 close = normal drift). Reading: **the vol pop is event-hedge demand INTO CPI Jul 10, plus oil supply shock signal — NOT rates-driven credit re-pricing.** This is important because rates-driven risk-off tends to be more sustained; event-hedge-driven vol tends to crush post-event.
- **Positioning + F&G + HY OAS data gap Week 5 now critical.** F&G stale Jun 17 = 3 weeks stale. NAAIM/COT/AAII gap continues. HY OAS not refreshed. **On a Stage-4 re-entry with cross-asset dispersion, credit read is now MISSION-CRITICAL** — if HY OAS quietly widened over the gap, cross-asset score could shift to -2, and Stage-5 tail probability could elevate further.
- **Geopolitical held +2 but is AT RISK.** MOU stable, no confirmed walk-back. But Brent +5.23% shock signal MAY suggest supply/geopolitical shock re-entering headline mix. If a named event confirms in next 24 hours (Iran/Russia/OPEC action), lens downgrades to 0 or -1 = total score -2 → -4 which materially elevates Stage-5 tail probability.

## TQQQ Read
- **Momentum:** RSI estimated ~40-44 — dropped decisively from ~52-55 Jul 6 on Jul 7 -5.31% estimated single-session decline. Approaching oversold zone (RSI 30) but not yet there. **Momentum backdrop: bearish shift, not yet oversold-actionable.** Path could compress further into CPI Jul 10.
